The Community House,
Grays, Essex.
RM17 6DR.
As always, our community cafes are completely free and for all people within the autistic and wider autism community i.e. Autistics of any age, parents, carers, other family members, professionals and any mix of the aforementioned.
The main aim of our community cafes are to provide the community a safe, inclusive and accessible place for people to come together, meet, connect and share.
There will be free tea, coffee, biscuits and other snacks, sensory items, games and more!
There's no need to register, just drop by.

description_short:
AS Mentoring supports clients to achieve their potential and facilitate social inclusion through employment.
-Provides pre-employment and workplace support.
-Offers coaching and mentoring for adults with Autism Spectrum Conditions and other neurodiverse conditions.
-Services available in London, Surrey and Birmingham, with remote options.
Description:
Our Mission is to support our clients to achieve their potential as individuals and to facilitate their social inclusion through employment.
https://asmentoring.co.uk/
There are a range of services including:
For Individuals:
Pre-employment support,
Workplace support and Coaching,
For Employers:
Recruitment, Consultancy, Management,
For Families, Individuals and Employers:
Various Workshops run throughout the year.
https://asmentoring.co.uk/what-we-do
Locations
The AS Mentoring team are based in 3 areas of the UK: London, Surrey and Birmingham.
We are very aware of the need for specialist support across the UK and further so we do work with clients remotely via Skype, phone-calls, email or a combination of all. If there is a need, staff can travel to other areas of the UK on an ad-hoc basis for Workplace Support and Coaching; this is something we'd be happy to discuss with you individually., Specialist Coaching and Mentoring for adults with Autism Spectrum Conditions and other neurodiverse conditions.

Working in Partnership
Future Ambitions wants to support organisations to help them engage with more young disabled people, if you would like more information please email future@scope.org.uk

description_short:
Uptimize courses teach you everything you need to know to impress employers. Get hired & advance your career with our online employability training.
-Online video training designed specifically to unlock the potential of the neurodiverse workforce.
-Our vision is world where neurodiversity is universally championed, and where organizations thrive thanks to their neurodiverse teams.
Description:
Neurodivergent individuals can bring unique and valuable skills to the workplace – from the attention to detail and data-driven thinking typical of people with autism, to the creativity and insight associated with people with dyslexia, dyspraxia and ADHD.
Uptimize courses teach you everything you need to know to impress employers. Get hired & advance your career with our online employability training.
Website: http://www.uptimize.com, Our best-in-class online video training is designed specifically to unlock the potential of the neurodiverse workforce. Our vision is world where neurodiversity is universally championed, and where organizations thrive thanks to their neurodiverse teams. We are excited to be on a journey and to be collaborating with other like-minded organizations, to make that vision a reality.

description_short:
We take a natural approach, getting to know jobseekers’ families, communities, and employers.
-Focuses on positive jobseeker attributes and employer adjustments.
-Works with individuals with autism, Asperger’s, or mild learning disabilities.
-Builds unique job-fit based on individual circumstances.
Description:
We take the most natural approach for each individual. We get to know their families, communities and employers within easy reach of home. We focus on what positive attributes our jobseekers can bring to the workplace whilst embracing what adjustments the employer might need to make to live in harmony with the autism or learning disability.
Our model of employment support puts the emphasis on getting the job-fit right. Each one of our jobseekers will have their own unique set of circumstances that will make up the best job-fit. And we believe we do a very good job understanding exactly what these are.
Email:richard@apotentialdiamond.org
Phone:077389 41415.
Richard Lamplough has had, and continues to have, a varied career. For much of the last twenty years he's used his creativity, drive and communication skills to support people with autism, Asperger's Syndrome or mild learning disabilities into paid employment., We work with people with autism or learning disabilities often while they are still at college but also if they are unemployed and keen to find full-time or part-time work. We get to know them as individuals, find out about their strengths and aspirations and build an understanding of what type of work and what sort of work environment will suit them best.

description_short:
Rural Care provides training and work experience for people with learning disabilities and/or mental health issues in a supportive and caring environment on a fully working farm.
-Rural Care provides training and work experience for people with learning disabilities and/or mental health issues in a supportive and caring environment on a fully working farm.
-Rural Care provides training and work experience for people with learning disabilities and/or mental health issues in a supportive and caring environment on a fully working farm.
-Rural Care provides training and work experience for people with learning disabilities and/or mental health issues in a supportive and caring environment on a fully working farm.
Description:
https://ruralcare.wordpress.com/
Aldenham Country Park
Aldenham Country Park consists of 100 acres of land surrounding a 60 acre lake. It has a farm with rare breed livestock such as Berkshire and British Lop pigs, Red Poll and Longhorn cattle, Black Welsh Mountain Sheep and free range poultry. All of this enables us at Rural Care to get involved in lots of different jobs and tasks., Rural Care provides training and work experience for people with learning disabilities and/or mental health issues in a supportive and caring environment on a fully working farm. We also work in partnership with North Hertfordshire College to provide accredited training for their students with learning disabilities on the farm. Volunteers are welcome and can help out with Rural Care or can work on their own so they can enjoy the tranquillity of the farm.

We help people to learn new skills and increase their confidence, through activity sessions at the centre or at community venues. We have minibuses to get to some of the venues or we use public transport.
Can we support you?
Yes, as long as you are over 18 years old and have autism or learning disabilities. You will need to pay for the activities and you can do that if you have your own funding or if you have been assessed for funding through your local council.
https://www.choicesupport.org.uk/find-support/find-support-near-you/carlton
Our activities
We decide the activities based on your feedback and your needs, and so that you can try new experiences.
We have a full programme of activities from cooking and computer skills to drama and drum beats. You will have a timetable of activities to choose from.
There is a small extra charge for some of our activities and we will tell you this when you join us., If you'd like us to support you and you have funding in place, we may be able to help you.

description_short:
Monthly support group for adults on the autistic spectrum living in the Greater London area. It is a chance to meet others to talk about common issues and to support and help each other.
-The first half-hour of the meeting is spent socialising and having refreshments.
-This is followed by the whole group sitting down together to discuss an agreed topic or to hear from different speakers...
Description:
ALAG runs a monthly support group for adults on the autistic spectrum living in the Greater London area although those from elsewhere are welcome. It is a chance to meet others to talk about common issues and to support and help each other. The first half-hour of the meeting is spent socialising and having refreshments. This is followed by the whole group sitting down together to discuss an agreed topic or to hear from different speakers who offer advice and guidance on issues relating to autism. These topics are planned in advance and any member can suggest a future topic.
The group meets on the first Tuesday of every month in Islington from 6.30pm to 8.30pm. Booking in advance for new attendees is essential. There is a small entrance fee of £3 (members) or £4 (non-members) to cover the cost of refreshments, including a vegetarian option.
Email: info@alag.org.uk
Telephone: 07435 560 568
Website: www.alag.org.uk

Description: https://www.meetup.com/London-Adults-with-mild-Autism-Spectrum-Disorder/, This meetup is for adults with Autism Spectrum Disorder, there is no need for a diagnosis if you feel/know that you have it then you are welcome. This group is intended for anyone who has ADHD, Autism, Dyslexia, Dyspraxia, Dyscalculia, Dysgraphia, and Tourette's syndrome within UK Professions.

-Membership will be open to other conditions that can be considered Neurodivergent.

Description: https://www.meetup.com/Neurodiversity-Professional-Group/, This group is intended for anyone who has ADHD, Autism, Dyslexia, Dyspraxia, Dyscalculia, Dysgraphia, and Tourette's syndrome within UK Professions. This is consistent with ACAS's view on Neurodiversity (http://www.acas.org.uk/index.aspx?articleid=6676) however, given this is a new concept, membership will be open to other conditions that can be considered Neurodivergent. The aim of this group twofold: (ii) to create greater connections within our community and (ii) to consider how we can address some of the challenges we face. Events to connect and network will be the main focus (details will be made available to members).

Description:

As part of our parent and family support services, Camden Special Parents’ Forum is a growing network of over 250 parents and carers of 0-25 year olds with special education needs and disabilities.

gmaps_name: City of London Parent Carer Forum
description_short:
City Parents & Carers is working with the City of London and the City and Hackney CCG to ensure that our voices are heard when decisions are being made which may affect our children and young people. 
Parents and carers of children and young people who have an Education Health and Care plan have been meeting once a term with local authority officers to talk about the services we would like to see for our children and young people.
Description:
City Parents & Carers
We are working with the City of London and the City and Hackney CCG to ensure that our voices are heard when decisions are being made which may affect our children and young people. We are represented on the City of London’s SEND Programme Board.
Parents and carers of children and young people who have an Education Health and Care plan have been meeting once a term with local authority officers to talk about the services we would like to see for our children and young people. We are developing and growing to include parents and carers of all children and young people with SEND regardless of whether they have an EHC plan.
As we grow we will be:
• Organising activities for our children and families
• Organising events and training days
• Linking with forums in other parts of the UK to share ideas
https://www.cityparentcarers.org

description_short:
Weekly group called Chill’n’Chats for parents and carers of children with special needs. The programme is decided by the parents, with a mix of speakers and parenting topics.
-Provides information, practical support and some relaxation time for parents.
-Current venues include Woodlands Children’s Centre in Selsdon and Kensington Avenue Children’s Centre.
-Child does not need to have a formal diagnosis for you to attend these sessions.
Description:
Chill’n’Chat Special is weekly group for parents and carers of children with special needs. We
recognise that all children are unique and they all develop and learn at different rates. Your
The Programme – To make this group as relevant as possible for those attending, the
programme is decided by the parents. We like to have a mixture of speakers from other
organisations, (National Autistic Society, Parents in Partnership, Paediatric Physiotherapists
and Parent Partnerships), parenting topics (building self-esteem, managing behaviour,
parenting styles etc.) and treats (crafts, card making, manicures, hand massages etc.).
Current Venues:-
Woodlands Children’s Centre, Selsdon CR2 8HD Tuesday morning 9.30-11.30
Kensington Avenue Children’s Centre, CR7 8BT – Wednesday afternoons 1.00-2.45
These groups meet term time only. Creche places may be available if booked in advance.
Jubilee Parenting - have been supporting parents in Croydon for about 12 years. We run a
range of parenting courses alongside our weekly Chill’n’Chats. We aim to provide information,
practical support and some relaxation time for parents who are often under pressure.
PRISM – is a consortium of organisations working together to support parents of children
with special needs.
If you would like more information or to be added to our email circulation, please contact
Jane Carey or Rachel Cummings
email: Jane.carey@croydon-jubilee.co.uk / Rachel.cummings@croydon-jubilee.co.uk, child does not need to have a formal diagnosis for you to attend these sessions.

General Telephone No: 020 8887 9980

http://www.enfieldmencap.org.uk/
Saturday Respite
This is a service providing respite to parents and carers supporting learning-disabled adults living at home in the London Borough of Enfield. The service is currently free at the point of delivery, being funded through the Enfield Carers’ Grant.
The service is provided every Saturday (excluding Easter, Christmas and New Year) 10:00 am–5:00 pm. Unlike other Enfield Mencap services, no transport to and from the service is provided.
Club 18+
A dedicated after-school/college club for young adults in transition from Children’s to Adult services. The Club currently operates from The New Opportunity Centre . It begins at 3:30pm and continues through to 7:00pm each evening.
Gateway Clubs
Enfield Mencap is pleased to support the three Gateway Clubs operating in the Borough.
The three Clubs operate in different parts of the Borough on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday evenings. They provide recreational facilities for people with learning disabilities., The New Opportunity Centre, The Community Hall, 1 Foxglove Close, Edmonton N9 8LW

description_short:
Parent–led organisation working with parents and carers, statutory and voluntary sectors.
-Improves services for children and young people with disabilities or special educational needs in Enfield.
-Negotiates representation on decision-making bodies.
-Campaigns to change attitudes and remove barriers.
Description:
We are a parent–led organisation, working with parents and carers, as well as the statutory and voluntary sectors.
Tel: 07516 662315
Email: info @ ourvoiceenfield.org.uk
Aims
Our Voice aims to improve services for children and young people with disabilities or special educational needs in the borough of Enfield.
To do this we:
We negotiate representation on decision-making bodies
We invite service providers to consult us on local and national issues affecting our children and seek a can-do approach from them
We ask children, parents and carers to tell us about common concerns
We inform & support families though our newsletters, website, workshops and meetings
We campaign to change attitudes and remove barriers
We monitor changes in services, lobby for improvements and recognise and promote good practices
We help empower all parents of children with special needs and disabilities
We offer the following services:
-Family Network support help line
-Disability Network Meetings
-Information Newsletters
-Consultancy Services; e.g. running Parent consultations, or providing Training for Parents and Professionals, https://www.ourvoiceenfield.org.uk

description_short: KEEN London is the only charity in London providing one-to-one support at free sports and activity sessions for children with additional needs or disabilities. We believe no child should miss out on the benefits of sports and physical activity. With the support of our amazing volunteers, our weekly sessions give our athletes the chance to play games, make friends and, most importantly, have fun!
Description:
KEEN London is the only charity in London providing one-to-one support at free sports and activity sessions for children with additional needs or disabilities. We believe no child should miss out on the benefits of sports and physical activity. With the support of our amazing volunteers, our weekly sessions give our athletes the chance to play games, make friends and, most importantly, have fun!
At our sessions, each athlete is paired with a trained volunteer coach for individual support. This means they benefit from one-to-one attention and care. It also means their parents can leave their children in a secure and safe environment.
For families, volunteers, supporters and athletes, this means the KEEN family is one of a kind: welcoming, safe, supportive, rewarding and fun.
https://www.keenlondon.org

Description
Harrow Mencap is an independent charity - affiliated to National Mencap.
Started by parents and carers in the late 1940's we became a limited company in 1987. We have grown into a multi-project service provider to support people with learning disabilities and their families. We are proud of our local reputation as the primary campaigning organisation for people with learning disabilities in Harrow.
Our Purpose:-to work with and represent people with learning disabilities and their families and carers to ensure their needs, rights and aspirations are met.
Harrow Mencap's Children's Services offer a variety of play schemes to include Saturdays during term time; 10am-2pm and holiday clubs outside of term time; 10am-3pm weekdays. Saturday clubs operate from our Day Centre at Pinner Green and holiday clubs at Kingsley High School, Whittlesea Road, Harrow, HA3 6ND.
From September, we will also be offering after school clubs at Kinglsey School Harrow, Shaftesbury School Harrow and Woodlands School Edgware, 3.30pm to 5.30pm. We also offer transport as part of the service . Please contact Julie Browne for more information on:
020 8423 7382
email: Julie.browne@harrowmencap.org.uk, Email:julie.browne@harrowmencap.org.uk

description_short:
Signal Family Support is a local parent support group, based in the London Borough of Lewisham, set up a number of years ago by parents of children who have autism, Asperger syndrome or other autism spectrum disorders (ASDs).
The group hold monthly meetings which include talks from experts on a variety of topics related to autism, including current research and educational provision.
Coffee mornings, held in different members houses, are open to all members to exchange ideas, experiences and information or just have a chat.
Description:
Signal Family Support, C/o Hatcham Oak Children’s Centre
Brockley
London
SE4 2NX
Tel: 07946 632477
http://www.signalfamilysupport.org
SIGNAL (Support, Information, Guidance & News for Autism in Lewisham) is a local parent support group, based in the London Borough of Lewisham, set up a number of years ago by parents of children who have autism, Asperger syndrome or other autism spectrum disorders (ASDs).
The group hold monthly meetings which include talks from experts on a variety of topics related to autism, including current research and educational provision.
Coffee mornings, held in different members houses, are open to all members to exchange ideas, experiences and information or just have a chat.
The group produces a quarterly newsletter and runs a borrowing library with many specialist books, journals and articles, videos, and specially selected children’s books. It maintains links with local schools, hold conferences for schools and teachers, organises drama therapy and trampolining activities, provides parent speakers for local functions to raise autism awareness and fundraises within the local community and through grants.
It also runs a 16 plus and a sibling support club, has an annual party, and runs and subsidises holiday outings and other activities for its membership., Wallbutton Road

Description: It is run for children with additional needs; for their siblings, parents, carers and grandparents too. The playgroup is completely free of charge., The Challenge Playgroup runs on Friday mornings in St. James' Church Hall, Muswell Hill. The happy noises of children playing are like those of hundreds of playgroups around the country. But this playgroup is special.

Impartial advice and information on education issues to parents/carers of children who experience barriers to learning such as special educational needs (SEN), disability, exclusion or bullying.
-Impartial advice and information on education issues.

Description:
The Web Building
Stratford
London
E15 4BQ

Tel: 0203 373 2871 or 020 3373 2869
www.newham.gov.uk
We provide impartial advice and information on education issues to parents/carers of children who experience barriers to learning such as special educational needs (SEN), disability, exclusion or bullying. What do we do?: We can attend and support parents/carers in meetings about their child’s education, and offer support if the parent/carer wishes to request a statutory assessment. We also help with writing letters and reports. Who is it for?: Parents/carers of children and young people who are attending school in Newham, or who attend an out of borough school if the child is a Newham resident. What age group does it cover?: We have a statutory responsibility to work with children and young people up to 25 years for those who have a Statement of Special Educational Need., 49-51 Broadway

(Special Educational Needs and Disabilities Information Advice and Support Service)
gmaps_formatted_address: 20 Windham Road
description_short:
Free, confidential and impartial service for parents and carers, children and young people (up to 25 years).
-Information regarding the local offer, local policy and SEN/ disability law.
-Advice for parent/carers, children and young people on gathering, understanding and interpreting information.
-Individual casework and representation where needed.
Description:
Richmond and Kingston Special Educational Needs and Disabilities Information Advice and Support Service or SENDIASS is a free, confidential and impartial service for parents and carers, children and young people (up to 25 years).
Richmond,
London,
TW92HP
Tel: 0208 831 6179
email: RichmondKingston@kids.org.uk
https://www.kids.org.uk/richmond-and-kingston-sendiass
The type and range of support offered includes:
-Information regarding the local offer, local policy and SEN/ disability law from independently trained staff.
-Advice for parent/carers, children and young people on gathering, understanding and interpreting information and applying it to their own situation.
-Personalisation of personal budgets
Information on the local authority’s processes for resolving disagreements, its complaints procedures and means of redress.
-Signposting children, young people and parents to alternative and additional sources of local and national information, advice and support.
Individual casework and representation where needed including support in attending meetings, contributing to assessments and reviews and participating in decisions about outcomes for the child or young person.
-Support for parents and young people in managing mediation and appeals to the SEND Tribunal., 20 Windham Road,

Description:
This is a club that aims to enhance social interactions and well-being for children with autism. Meets at Westfield School on Monday evenings 5.00-6.45pm, term time only.
Suitable for children on the autism spectrum and their siblings; up to the age of 11.
We pride ourselves on offering a friendly environment for children and their families.
We aim to offer a wide variety of activities to promote positive interactions, self-esteem and well-being.
Activities include board games, arts & crafts, toys including trains sets, PE equipment in hall and many more things.
An opportunity to meet other parents and have a coffee.
Suggested voluntary donation of £4.00 per family includes a drink and a snack.

description_short:
Group music therapy for children aged between 5-13 with special needs and disabilities.
-Music therapy sessions will take place on every 2nd Saturday morning of the month at In-Deep’s Grosvenor Hall Vincent Street venue in Victoria between 11am-12.30pm.
-Sessions are run by a qualified, experienced therapist.
-Music therapy has long been shown to help children communicate and help develop speech and further physical, emotional and mental development.
Description:
The sessions will be supported by a group of trained volunteers and are run by a qualified, experienced therapist.
Alongside the sessions, In-Deep will be providing parents a place where they can meet and share their experiences with other parents and carers; as many parents who care for a child with special needs and/or disabilities can often find themselves feeling isolated, overwhelmed and alone.
Music therapy has long been shown to help children communicate and help develop speech and further physical, emotional and mental development.
If your child has learning difficulties or disabilities just bring them along to one of our sessions all children will be welcomed and catered for. For more information contact Emma Chapman by using the email:
emma.chapman@in-deep.org.uk or by calling: tel: 07761263105 or 02078345204.
http://in-deep.org.uk/activitie/childrens-music-therapy/, In-Deep offers group music therapy for children aged between 5-13 with special needs and disabilities. These music therapy sessions will take place on every 2nd Saturday morning of the month at In-Deep’s Grosvenor Hall Vincent Street venue in Victoria between 11am-12.30pm.

Description:

AIM Autistic Community Group

description_short:
Living Services offering all year round support in people’s own or family homes and within single or shared accommodations.
-Provides support in single or shared accommodations.
-Offers rented single person and shared accommodation, within small homes for up to six people.
-Supports people through Supporting People funding with Steady State Contracts with Local Authorities.
Description:
Living Services
Some of our homes are Registered with the Care Quality Commission and regular inspection reports are available on the Care Quality Commission website.
We also provide support for people from self-contained flats in shared accommodation with communal areas with the levels of staff determined by the individuals who live there. Staff can be shared to enable a higher overall level of support. This type of support is especially cost effective and flexible for people who want less support.
For people who are supported through Supporting People funding we have Steady State Contracts with Local Authorities and are required to adhere to a Quality Assessment Framework.
However a person chooses to get their support from the Trust we believe in an individual, personal approach. Providing sensitive support at the level determined by each individual’s need. We use person centred approaches and work with each person to find the right balance of positive aid for maximum independence, choice and determination. We believe that each person can tell us what they want in their own home and we work hard to ensure that we have all the mechanisms in place to make sure we hear what people tell us whatever their method of communication.
For more detailed information about our support services please visit this section of our website:
https://www.kentautistic.com/what-we-do/living-services/
Contact details:
Tel: 01634 405168
email: office@kentautistic.com, The Trust offers all year round living support. We can provide this in people’s own or family homes and within single or shared accommodations. We are able to provide rented single person and shared accommodation, within small homes for up to six people.

Description: http://cyclingforall.org/cycling-sessions/, We are open seven days a week in Dulwich Park for general bike hire and Monday to Friday for sales.Our cycling facilities are for park users who want some gentle excersise and a bit of fun, or to let the kids burn off some steam after a week of being cooped up.We can deal with larger groups, a birthday party say, or a social group, or a school group (of just about ANY sort) and we often hire special bikes to adland.1. for individuals & families.Turn up and hire, its that simple!. description_short:
Falcon Spartak Gymnastics Club offers inclusive gymnastics sessions for children with various impairments including Amputee, Learning Disabilities, and Visual Impairment. Sessions are held on Wednesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays. All new gymnasts are allowed 3 taster sessions before joining the club.
-Inclusive sessions for children with disabilities.
-Sessions held on Wednesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays.
-Disabilities catered for: Amputee, Learning Disabilities, Visual Impairment.
Description:
Hurstmere School, Hurst Road,
Sidcup,
Kent,
Kent,
DA15 9AW
Telephone Number
07967 346060
Email Address
kay.salter@btopenworld.com
Websites
www.falconspartak.com
Inclusive sessions include disabled participants, ensuring that additional help and support is provided when and if required. Dedicated sessions follow the inclusive gym class but allowing for 1:1 coach support work where it is required. Use of special adapted equipment as well as mainstream gymnastics equipment is used to enable all ability levels to participate and access gymnastics. Falcon Spartak allow carers and parents to assist in the gym. They can leave as and when they are happy to. Gymnasts can also be given a buddy to work with. All new gymnasts are allowed 3 taster sessions before the final decision is taken to join the club. this allows the gymnast and parent/carers time to decide if they like the club. Disabled Gymnasts have the opportunity to compete in regional and club competitions, displays, GymFusion, Disability National events in Rhythmic, Artistic and Trampolining. All coaches in the club have taken the disability add-on module (3 club coaches, 1 assistant coach, 1 trampoline coach and 1 senior club coach in Rhythmic) Club competitions take place before Christmas. The club also celebrates its 40th birthday in 2013 so there will be events to celebrate this. All events held at the club are fully inclusive and gymnasts from dedicated sessions are also encouraged to attend.
Sports Offered
Gymnastics
Impairments catered for
Amputee, Learning Disabilities, Visual Impairment.
When do we meet
Wednesday 6.30pm to 8.30pm, Thursday 6.30 to 8.30pm, Saturday 12.30pm to 2.30pm, Falcon Spartak Gymnastics Club

description_short:
Therapeutic choir ran by an occupational therapist, music specialist and medical doctor. Through the team’s love of music and experience of working with children with additional needs this project has been born.
Inclusive notes is the first of its kind to use singing in a choir to help children develop their skills and achieve their therapeutic goals.
Children will have fun, enjoy making music and succeed in a safe, accepting and engaging environment.
We aim to work with you and your child to mould the experience around your child’s needs.
Description:
Inclusive notes is a therapeutic choir ran by an occupational therapist, music specialist and medical doctor. Meet the team here. Through the team’s love of music and experience of working with children with additional needs this project has been born.
Inclusive notes is the first of its kind to use singing in a choir to help children develop their skills and achieve their therapeutic goals. Children will have fun, enjoy making music and succeed in a safe, accepting and engaging environment.
We aim to work with you and your child to mould the experience around your child’s needs. Continued assessment will be in place in order to frame the achievement of therapeutic goals.
The team will use both their musical and professional skills to provide sessions that are inclusive and fun, and that are differentiated to match each child’s ability to make every session a ‘just right’ challenge for your child.
https://www.inclusivenotes.com
Please note due to the nature of the choir, no drop-ins will be permitted. Please get in touch to arrange a phone consultation and free trial on inclusivenotes@gmail.com.
Dates arranged via direct contact.
Time: 5-6pm
Venue: Whitmore Community Centre, 2-4 Phillip Street. Hackney N1 5NU
Cost: £100 for an 11 week term, A specialist choir in Hackney for children aged 6-11 with additional needs.

description_short: Wormwood Scrubs RDA is a centre catering for children and young people with physical disabilities and learning difficulties. Children from local special needs schools attend for riding and other forms of equine therapy. Individual children and out of school groups also attend. The centre has an indoor arena and a stable yard therapy classroom.
Description:
Tel No. 020 8740 0573
Email: wormwoodscrubsponycentre@gmail.com
http://www.wormwoodscrubsponycentre.org/RIDING-FOR-THE-DISABLED.html, Wormwood Scrubs RDA is situated in West London. The Centre caters for children and young people with physical disabilities and learning difficulties. During the week children from local special needs schools come to the Centre for riding and other forms of equine therapy. On Saturdays individual children attend as well as out of school groups. Our ponies can carry up to 9st in weight. The Centre has an indoor arena and a unique stable yard therapy classroom which facilitates many learning tools and a dressage horse simulator. The centre has in the region of 60 volunteers who kindly help in many different ways. All volunteers who wish to help with the ponies and riders are given necessary training. If you are interested in booking in a school group or paying a visit to the centre please send an email.

description_short:
AutAngel is running community Shintaido sessions; open to all.
-Improve non-verbal communication and physical co-ordination.
-Autism-friendly Shintaido class in Reading.
-Classes on Wednesday evenings: 7.30pm to 9pm
Description:
Improve non-verbal communication and physical co-ordination.
Shintaido is a modern movement system based on traditional Japanese martial arts with the emphasis on self-development and artistic-expression rather than self-defence. It offers direct training in non-verbal communication helps with co-ordination and can lead to other positive life changes over time.
AutAngel is running community Shintaido sessions; open to all. Charles Burns, our experienced and qualified instructor, has Asperger’s Syndrome and has found Shintaido enormously helpful in overcoming various problems associated with this condition.
Classes on Wednesday evenings: 7.30pm to 9pm
For more information or to register for the ongoing sessions:
email charles@shintaido.co.uk
or call him on 07803 085637., Autism-friendly Shintaido class in Reading.

Description:
Knots Arts drama sessions run in East Sheen and are an exciting place for children aged 4 – 16 years old with social communication difficulties to come and make new friends whilst having fun.

Drama sessions consist of games and activities which are designed to support the development of team working, processing and understanding and speech and language. Each session begins with a sharing of news from our week, followed by drama games appropriate to the ability of the group and culminating in a drama activity such as story-telling, physical theatre or improvisation.
